search_terms_used: VO2max determinants endurance performance systematic review Bassett Howley 2000 maximal oxygen uptake pubmed
VO2max trainability genetics individual variation endurance training response HERITAGE study pubmed
Joyner Coyle 2008 endurance exercise performance physiology VO2max lactate threshold running economy review
VO2max training improvement timeline weeks plateau endurance athletes systematic review meta-analysis pubmed
cardiac output stroke volume VO2max limiting factor central peripheral oxygen transport endurance review pubmed
Montero Lundby 2017 refuting VO2max oxygen delivery cardiac output haematological adaptations pubmed
Wagner 1996 determinants maximal oxygen transport utilization annual review physiology VO2max pubmed
Wenger Bell 1986 aerobic power training rate improvement VO2max meta-analysis review pubmed
coverage_notes: Strong coverage of VO2max determinants and limits: oxygen delivery vs peripheral limitation debate (Bassett & Howley 2000, Saltin & Strange 1992, Wagner 1996), haematological adaptations (Lundby 2017), cardiac remodeling (Levine 2008), integrative performance model (Joyner & Coyle 2008), genetic trainability (Bouchard HERITAGE 1999), training prescription (Wenger & Bell 1986), overview of training intensity effects (Crowley 2022 umbrella review), and aging decline (Hawkins & Wiswell 2003). Good balance of foundational and contemporary work.

papers.title: The interactions of intensity, frequency and duration of exercise training in altering cardiorespiratory fitness
papers.notes: Reviewed how exercise intensity, frequency, and duration interact to alter cardiorespiratory fitness, finding greatest VO2max gains at 90-100% VO2max intensity.

papers.title: Maximal oxygen uptake: "old" and "new" arguments for a cardiovascular limitation
papers.notes: Presented classical and contemporary arguments that cardiovascular oxygen delivery is the primary limitation for VO2max during whole-body exercise.

papers.title: Determinants of maximal oxygen transport and utilization
papers.notes: Modeled the four critical steps in the O2 transport pathway showing how VO2max depends on ventilation, pulmonary diffusion, circulation, and muscle diffusion.

papers.title: Familial aggregation of VO(2max) response to exercise training: results from the HERITAGE Family Study
papers.notes: Examined familial aggregation of VO2max training response in 481 sedentary adults from 98 families, finding 47% heritability for VO2max trainability.

papers.title: Limiting factors for maximum oxygen uptake and determinants of endurance performance
papers.notes: Reviewed evidence that O2 delivery (not skeletal muscle extraction) is the primary limiter of VO2max, supported by blood doping, hypoxia, and beta-blockade studies.

papers.title: Rate and mechanism of maximal oxygen consumption decline with aging: implications for exercise training
papers.notes: Reviewed rate and mechanisms of VO2max decline with aging, finding approximately 10% per decade decline regardless of activity level.

papers.title: VO2max: what do we know, and what do we still need to know?
papers.notes: Reviewed VO2max as a true parametric measure bounded by the Fick equation, emphasizing enlarged compliant cardiac chambers and greater stroke volume in elite athletes.

papers.title: Endurance exercise performance: the physiology of champions
papers.notes: Reviewed how VO2max, lactate threshold, and efficiency interact to determine endurance performance, with VO2max and LT determining 'performance VO2'.

papers.title: Biology of VO2max: looking under the physiology lamp
papers.notes: Reviewed how red blood cell volume expansion and stroke volume improvements drive training-induced VO2max gains, emphasizing haematological adaptations.

papers.title: The Effect of Exercise Training Intensity on VO2max in Healthy Adults: An Overview of Systematic Reviews and Meta-Analyses
papers.notes: Umbrella review of systematic reviews and meta-analyses examining the effect of exercise training intensity on VO2max improvements in healthy adults.
